content:
- Updated the task file to <status>done</status> and <passes>true</passes>, checked off the acceptance criteria, and checked off the execution slices in the plan.
- Ran .ralph/task_switch.sh, which updated the Ralph task tracking files, removed current_task.txt, and recorded the finished-task notification.
- Next I am making the required final task-finished commit with local and remote verification evidence, pushing it, and then stopping immediately.

- Started Choose-Task turn by reading the list-active-tasks skill and prior progress entry.
- Confirmed the previous turn finished a task and switched Ralph tracking state.
- Next I am refreshing the Ralph task summary and reading current task indexes plus choose-task rules.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and resolved all active blockers against current_tasks_done.
- Confirmed the repo is a Go service (`go.mod`, `Makefile`) with internal packages for app/config/http/database/benchmark layers and only one unblocked failing task remains.
- Selecting .ralph/tasks/story-08-release-docs/task-02-quickstart-docs.md as the next task, then writing it to .ralph/current_task.txt and quitting.

- Read prior Ralph progress log, current task pointer, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skills.
- Confirmed current task path is .ralph/tasks/story-08-release-docs/task-02-quickstart-docs.md.
- Next I am reading the task file and nearby story context to determine whether this turn is plan-only or execution.

- Read the quickstart docs task and confirmed it is non-code, not_started, and currently lacks a plan reference.
- The task instructions require a separate planning file first when there is no existing execution marker.
- Next I am inspecting the blocker task state and the existing plan-file format in this story so I can add the new plan in the expected shape and then stop.

- Confirmed the blocker task already passes, so the quickstart docs task is available to plan.
- I am now reading only the repo surfaces that the docs must describe: current docs layout, config examples, Docker Compose assets, and the HTTP/metrics endpoints.
- The goal is to make the plan specific enough for honest execution without prematurely editing docs in this planning turn.

- Found the concrete surfaces this docs task must cover: compose example config, compose startup file, and the HTTP server routes.
- The plan now needs to specify where the quickstart lives, which existing example files it should reuse, and which commands must be executed for manual verification.
- I am reading those source files directly before writing the plan.

- Confirmed there is no root README today, which makes the quickstart file choice part of the actual interface design for this task.
- I am reading the config loader and benchmark result/metrics shapes so the plan can call out the exact fields and stats that the docs need to explain.
- After that I will write the planning file and update the task with its plan path.

- Read the config and benchmark surface definitions; the remaining planning gap is the exact CLI/container startup contract plus a realistic TLS-path example.
- I am using existing app, Dockerfile, and config fixtures so the plan does not invent documentation that the binary cannot actually support.
- After this pass I should have enough context to create the plan file and patch the task with its plan path.

- The plan now has enough repo context except for the exact benchmark request payloads that the docs must show.
- I am reading the public benchmark option model and HTTP server tests to anchor the start/alter examples in real accepted fields.
- Then I will write the plan file, update the task with its <plan> path, and stop as required.

- Created the planning file for the quickstart docs task at .ralph/tasks/story-08-release-docs/task-02-quickstart-docs_plans/2026-04-30-quickstart-docs-plan.md.
- Updated .ralph/tasks/story-08-release-docs/task-02-quickstart-docs.md to include the new <plan> path.
- The plan is intentionally left at TO BE VERIFIED and this turn stops here per task instructions; next turn should review/promote the plan to NOW EXECUTE before implementing docs and running verification commands.

- Read prior Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the required improve-code-boundaries and tdd skill instructions.
- Confirmed current task is .ralph/tasks/story-08-release-docs/task-02-quickstart-docs.md and prior turn ended after creating a plan.
- Next I am reading the task file and the referenced plan to determine whether to promote the plan or execute it.

- Read the quickstart docs task and its existing planning file ending in TO BE VERIFIED.
- Verified the plan against the real repo surfaces: Docker Compose example, example config, HTTP routes, and accepted benchmark payload fields.
- Promoted .ralph/tasks/story-08-release-docs/task-02-quickstart-docs_plans/2026-04-30-quickstart-docs-plan.md from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE.
- Next turn should read the promoted plan, execute the documentation work, and run make check, make lint, make test, plus the required manual Docker and curl verification.

- Read prior progress and confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/story-08-release-docs/task-02-quickstart-docs.md.
- Re-read the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skills; prior turn already promoted the quickstart-docs plan to NOW EXECUTE.
- Next I am reading the task and execution plan, then I will implement the docs changes and run the required verification lanes.

- Read the task, execution plan, compose example, config loader, CLI flags, HTTP handlers, results, metrics, and k8s README.
- Confirmed the docs task is a TDD exception; verification must be command execution, not tests that assert file text.
- Next I am writing the canonical root README and likely trimming duplicated generic API walkthrough text from the k8s README to preserve a cleaner docs boundary.

- Added the canonical root README quickstart and reduced duplicated generic benchmark walkthrough text in examples/k8s/README.md.
- The docs now describe YAML-only config, credential modes, compose startup, direct scratch-image execution, control-plane curl calls, and stats/metrics interpretation.
- Next I am validating the markdown for obvious mistakes, then I will run make check, make lint, make test, and the manual Docker verification commands.

- All required validation passed: make check, make lint, make test, plus real Docker Compose and scratch-image quickstart verification.
- The documented compose startup, health/readiness/state/results/metrics calls, benchmark start/alter/stop flow, and direct scratch-image run all succeeded.
- Next I am marking the task artifacts complete, switching Ralph state, committing all files, and pushing.

- Marked the quickstart docs task and execution plan complete after all gates and manual verification passed.
- The boundary cleanup kept the root README canonical and reduced duplicate generic benchmark instructions in the Kubernetes example.
- Next I am running Ralph task switch bookkeeping, then I will git add all files, commit with validation evidence, push, and stop immediately.
